varieties.name: Angel Subtropical (UFO / Fla.98-7CP)
varieties.origin: University of Florida breeding program, 1990s
varieties.traits: Novel flat/donut-shaped fruit, medium size, distinctive appearance
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow flesh, freestone, very sweet soft and juicy texture, superior to standard peaches
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields, approximately 150 chill hours required
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for Brisbane subtropical climate, flowers early in late winter, grows to 3m x 3m, suitable from Rockhampton south through subtropical Queensland and coastal NSW. Pink spring flowers.
varieties.availability: PlantNet Queensland nurseries, Daleys Fruit Tree Nursery

varieties.name: China Flat
varieties.origin: China, brought to Australia in 1800s gold rush era, heirloom variety
varieties.traits: Flat/squat UFO shape, pale colored skin, medium to large size
varieties.flesh_fruit: White flesh, freestone, deliciously sweet flavor, juicy
varieties.yield_notes: Prolific bearer, 150 chill hours only
varieties.growing_notes: Old Australian heirloom brought by Chinese immigrants. Perfect for subtropical Brisbane, arguably sweetest flavor of all peaches. Self-pollinating, grows to 4m (easily pruned to 2m). Fruits in 12-18 months. Historic significance to Queensland peach culture.
varieties.availability: Daleys Fruit Tree Nursery, Heritage Fruit Trees, Ross Creek Tropicals, Ladybird Nursery, Heaven On Earth Fruit Trees

varieties.name: Flordaprince (Florida Prince)
varieties.origin: University of Florida, released 1982
varieties.traits: Large fruit with 80% red blush over yellow background, dark red stripes
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow melting flesh, semi-clingstone pit, uniformly firm, highly aromatic flavor, excellent taste
varieties.yield_notes: Early season, very productive, 150 chill hours, ripens November-December in Brisbane

varieties.name: TropicBeauty
varieties.origin: University of Florida & Texas A&M, released 1989
varieties.traits: Medium sized, round firm fruit, high red overcolor on bright yellow, very short fuzz making highly attractive
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep yellow melting flesh, frees from pit at soft ripe, sweet with excellent flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive, 150 chill hours, holds well on tree, late April ripening (December in Brisbane)

varieties.name: TropicSnow
varieties.origin: University of Florida & Texas A&M, released 1989
varieties.traits: Medium to large, 40-50% red blush over creamy white background
varieties.flesh_fruit: White melting flesh, semi-freestone, very low acid combined with excellent sweetness, creamy white non-browning flesh
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer, 225 chill hours, FDP 90-97 days
varieties.growing_notes: Outstanding flavor profile for Brisbane. Round medium to large fruit. Suited to subtropical coastal regions Sydney to Nambour Queensland. Highly resistant to bacterial spot. November-December harvest.
varieties.availability: Daleys Fruit Tree Nursery, Queensland suppliers

varieties.name: Flordaglo
varieties.origin: University of Florida, released 1988
varieties.traits: 50-60% red blush with stripes over white ground color
varieties.flesh_fruit: White melting flesh, semi-clingstone, sweet with low acid
varieties.yield_notes: Early ripening, productive, 150 chill hours, FDP 78 days
varieties.growing_notes: Bacterial spot resistant. Ideal for backyard Brisbane orchards though fruit shows bruises easily (handle carefully). Melting flesh texture. Ripens early May (November in Brisbane).
varieties.availability: Queensland fruit tree nurseries

varieties.name: Aztec Gold
varieties.origin: Low-chill breeding program
varieties.traits: Large fruit, red/yellow attractive skin
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow flesh, firm non-melting, excellent flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer, 300 chill hours

varieties.name: Early Beauty (Tropical Sensations®)
varieties.origin: PlantNet breeding program, Australia
varieties.traits: Medium clingstone fruit, yellow flesh with red pigments, dark red over yellow skin
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow flesh, clingstone, excellent quality
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields, low chill requirement, harvest September-October
varieties.growing_notes: Part of Tropical Sensations® range specifically for subtropical/coastal climates. Suitable far north as Rockhampton. Peach rust resistant with great shelf life. Excellent fruit quality achievable by home gardeners. Full sun preferred with afternoon shade in hot climates.
varieties.availability: PlantNet stockists in Queensland, WA garden centers

varieties.name: Super Dwarf Sunset Peach™ (Tropical Sensations®)
varieties.origin: PlantNet breeding program (Fla. 03-01ordw)
varieties.traits: Dwarf tree 1.5-2m, compact growth, medium clingstone fruit, red skin, white flesh
varieties.flesh_fruit: White flesh, clingstone, good flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Low chill suitable for subtropical AND temperate climates, November-December (subtropical) harvest
varieties.growing_notes: Great for Brisbane small gardens and containers. Dwarf habit perfect for limited space. Can be grown Australia-wide with appropriate rootstock. Double pink blossoms in spring. Available on low-chill rootstock for Queensland.
varieties.availability: PlantNet online shop, Queensland nurseries

varieties.name: Sunset Backyard Beauty (Dwarf)
varieties.origin: Backyard Beauties™ range, PlantNet
varieties.traits: Dwarf variety, compact to 2m, pink flowers in July, white flesh peach
varieties.flesh_fruit: White flesh, freestone, full-sized fruit despite dwarf tree
varieties.yield_notes: Low chill, mature late December (Christmas harvest), good producer for size
varieties.growing_notes: Perfect for subtropical regions Brisbane and beyond. Ideal for pots, small backyards. Grafted on dwarfing rootstock (60cm high when sold). Very attractive ornamental with pink flowers. Easy to net for fruit fly protection.
varieties.availability: Daleys Fruit Tree Nursery

varieties.name: UFGold
varieties.origin: University of Florida, released 1996
varieties.traits: Large symmetrical fruit, 70-90% blush over orange-yellow ground, attractive appearance
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow non-melting flesh, clingstone, firm texture, excellent for longer shelf life
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy annual crops, 200 chill hours, FDP 80 days, early May ripening (December Brisbane)

varieties.name: Flordasun
varieties.origin: University of Florida low-chill program
varieties.traits: Medium round fruit, firm skin, good blush, very good skin color
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow flesh, semi-clingstone, very firm with excellent flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Earliest fruiting peach, 150 chill hours, 81 days from bloom, popular low-chill variety
varieties.growing_notes: One of the best subtropical peach selections. Earliest harvest of season. Extremely popular in Queensland and coastal NSW. Proven performer in Brisbane's climate.
varieties.availability: Daleys Fruit Tree Nursery, widespread availability

varieties.name: Flordagold
varieties.origin: University of Florida breeding program
varieties.traits: Medium to large fruit, attractive yellow/red coloring
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow flesh, good quality, up to 325 chill hours
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer, mid-season variety
varieties.growing_notes: Suitable for subtropical Brisbane with slightly higher chill tolerance. Mid-season ripening extends harvest window. Excellent quality fruit.
varieties.availability: Queensland nurseries

varieties.name: Newbelle
varieties.origin: Florida low-chill breeding program
varieties.traits: Medium fruit size, attractive appearance
varieties.flesh_fruit: White flesh, good flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable producer, low chill requirement suitable for subtropical climates
varieties.growing_notes: Selected for subtropical coastal areas. Performs well in Brisbane's warm humid conditions. Part of Florida's extensive low-chill breeding program specifically for warm winter regions.
varieties.availability: Queensland specialty nurseries

varieties.name: Anzac
varieties.origin: Released 1915, Australia - named in honor of ANZAC forces
varieties.traits: Large white freestone peach, nice pink blush over creamy-white skin
varieties.flesh_fruit: White flesh, freestone, classic excellent peach flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields, medium chill, late December to January harvest
varieties.growing_notes: Older Australian heirloom variety (110 years old). Classic peach with consistent flavor. Does well in most parts of Australia except extreme subtropical/tropical. Historical significance. Requires thinning for best fruit size.
varieties.availability: PlantNet nurseries, heritage fruit tree specialists

varieties.name: Indian Free (Red Indian)
varieties.origin: Heirloom Australian variety, approximately 100 years old, 1920s
varieties.traits: White flesh with ruby red skin, attractive coloration
varieties.flesh_fruit: White flesh, freestone, amazingly sweet, abundantly productive
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy producer, mid-January harvest for about a month
varieties.growing_notes: Old Australian heirloom variety with century-long history. Less susceptible to leaf curl. Self-fertile. Renowned for exceptional sweetness. One of Australia's traditional varieties. Continuous harvest over 4+ weeks.
varieties.availability: Daleys Fruit Tree Nursery, Woodbridge Fruit Trees, Heritage collections

varieties.name: Fresno
varieties.origin: California, USA
varieties.traits: Large attractive yellow-fleshed peach
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow flesh, freestone, excellent flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer, late December harvest

varieties.name: Golden Queen (Four Seasons Peach)
varieties.origin: Australian selection
varieties.traits: Large clingstone fruit, white flesh with red over white/yellow skin, extremely ornamental qualities
varieties.flesh_fruit: White flesh, clingstone, excellent aromatic flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Medium chill, consistent producer
varieties.growing_notes: Nicknamed 'Four Seasons Peach' - stunning pink spring flowers followed by rich yellow leaves, bright winter growth maintains year-round interest. Easy to grow with consistent flavor. Ornamental value. PBR protected. Suitable most regions except subtropical/tropical.
varieties.availability: PlantNet nurseries

varieties.name: Crimson Rocket (Columnar)
varieties.origin: Modern breeding for space-saving
varieties.traits: Medium to large freestone, deep red skin over yellow, distinctive columnar growth habit
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow flesh, freestone, excellent fruit quality
varieties.yield_notes: Medium chill, good production despite compact form

varieties.name: White Gold™
varieties.origin: PlantNet breeding program
varieties.traits: White fleshed variety with attractive appearance
varieties.flesh_fruit: White flesh, good quality
varieties.yield_notes: Medium chill requirement, reliable producer

varieties.name: Shan-e-Punjab 🌏
varieties.origin: Punjab, India - popular commercial cultivar for subtropical regions
varieties.traits: Large fruit, yellow-colored with reddish blush, attractive appearance, maximum length 5.51cm
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow flesh, freestone, succulent, superb flavor, firm quality, maximum weight 58.59g
varieties.yield_notes: High yield up to 21.39 kg per tree, matures first week of May, early season
varieties.growing_notes: One of most popular commercially cultivated varieties in India, particularly Punjab and Bihar. LOW-CHILL variety specifically suited to subtropical regions like Jammu and Punjab. Can tolerate transportation. Good for canning. Indigenous to Indian conditions. Regular yield of 70kg/tree. Available as grafted plants.
varieties.availability: Punjab Agricultural University, Indian nurseries, Ixora Farming, agricultural extension services Kerala

varieties.name: Prabhat (Saharanpur Prabhat) 🌏
varieties.origin: Saharanpur, India - local selection for subtropical plains
varieties.traits: Medium fruit, attractive appearance
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good flesh quality, richest source of riboflavin (15.43 μg/g) and thiamine (3.42 μg/g), good niacin content
varieties.yield_notes: Average yield 64 kg per tree, matures third week of April, early season
varieties.growing_notes: Popular variety throughout India. Low-chill selection suitable for subtropical areas. Excellent nutritional profile makes it valuable for fresh consumption. Grown in Jammu & Kashmir, low hills and plains. High vitamin content.
varieties.availability: Indian agricultural universities, northern India nurseries, potentially Kerala agricultural departments

varieties.name: Pratap (Partap) 🌏
varieties.origin: Punjab/North India - widely cultivated local variety
varieties.traits: Yellow colored with red blush, attractive fruit
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow flesh, better firmness compared to others, good riboflavin content (13.53 μg/g)
varieties.yield_notes: High yield potential - up to 17 kg per tree under favorable conditions, average 70 kg/plant, matures third week of April
varieties.growing_notes: One of highest yielding varieties in India. Maturity reaches in 76 days from full bloom. Often grown as grafted plant to enhance characteristics. Noted for its high yield reports. Found in Punjab and North India. Early bearer - harvesting by end of April.
varieties.availability: Punjab nurseries, North Indian agricultural stations, Indian mail-order nurseries

varieties.name: Flordaprince (Florida Prince)
varieties.origin: University of Florida 1982, introduced to India PAU Ludhiana 1968
varieties.traits: Large attractive fruit, 80% red blush over yellow, firm semi-clingstone
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow melting flesh, highly aromatic flavor, excellent taste, good for fresh consumption
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive, 150 chill hours, average yield 100kg per tree, matures fourth week of April

varieties.name: Flordasun
varieties.origin: University of Florida, introduced to India
varieties.traits: Medium round fruit, firm skin, good blush
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow flesh, semi-clingstone, very firm, excellent flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Earliest fruiting peach, 150 chill hours, 81 days from bloom, popular variety
varieties.growing_notes: Successfully grown in India's subtropical regions. Popular in low hills, plains, and Punjab. Earliest harvest of season. Selected from extensive Florida breeding programs. Widely available in Indian nursery systems.
varieties.availability: Indian agricultural nurseries, Punjab sources

varieties.name: Earligrande
varieties.origin: Developed for Indian subtropics
varieties.traits: Large fruit with excellent color, highest pulp:stone ratio (6.38) with 79.40g pulp weight
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good flesh quality, maximum TSS (18.86°B), lowest acidity (0.18%), highest total sugar content
varieties.yield_notes: 40% more yield than Shan-i-Punjab, average 95 kg per tree, 73 days from full bloom, early bearer
varieties.growing_notes: Proved superior to Shan-i-Punjab in trials. Fruits mature slightly earlier. Can be transported to distant markets. Similar disease resistance to Flordasun. Developed specifically for subtropical India. Best pulp to stone ratio of tested varieties. Ideal for fresh consumption due to sweetness.
varieties.availability: ICAR research stations, Indian horticultural centers

varieties.name: Sharbati 🌏
varieties.origin: India - local selection, traditional variety
varieties.traits: Large fruit, greenish-yellow colored with rosy patches
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very juicy, nice flavored, rich in antioxidant phenolic compounds, good ascorbic acid (330 μg/g), good riboflavin and thiamine content
varieties.yield_notes: Average yield 100-120 kg per tree, fruits ripen June-end to first week of July, late season
varieties.growing_notes: Local Indian variety grown in Punjab, plains areas. One of highest yielding traditional varieties. Later ripening extends season. Distinctly significant cultivar for antioxidants. Ideal for fresh consumption. Traditional Indian selection with cultural significance.
varieties.availability: Punjab nurseries, northern India agricultural sources

varieties.name: Sharbati Surkha 🌏
varieties.origin: India - Sharbati selection
varieties.traits: Red-fleshed selection of Sharbati
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good flesh quality, distinctly significant for antioxidant phenolic compounds, rich in ascorbic acid (310 μg/g), stands out for flavor-imparting organic acids
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer, used in hybridization programs
varieties.growing_notes: Best overall cultivar - contains all three groups of nutraceuticals in sufficient amounts for per capita nutrition. Used as female parent in breeding programs. Distinctly significant for phenolic compounds and vitamins. Ideal for fresh consumption and nutritional benefits.
varieties.availability: ICAR research institutes, specialized Indian nurseries

varieties.name: Pant Peach-1 🌏
varieties.origin: G.B. Pant University, Pantnagar, Uttarakhand, India
varieties.traits: Developed for Indian conditions
varieties.flesh_fruit: Rich in ascorbic acid (300 μg/g), good niacin content (27.23 μg/g), stands out for organic acids giving excellent flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer from university breeding
varieties.growing_notes: Developed by prestigious G.B. Pant University breeding program specifically for Indian growing conditions. Rich source of immune-enhancing vitamins. Similar to Pratap for organic acid content. Modern Indian breeding achievement.
varieties.availability: G.B. Pant University, Uttarakhand horticultural departments

varieties.name: Khurmani 🌏
varieties.origin: India - local traditional variety, grown in Punjab
varieties.traits: Large attractive fruit with red coloration
varieties.flesh_fruit: White soft juicy flesh, clingstone
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer in Punjab region
varieties.growing_notes: Traditional variety grown in Punjab and northern regions. White-fleshed clingstone type. Local selection adapted to Indian subtropical conditions. Part of traditional Indian peach heritage.
varieties.availability: Punjab local nurseries, traditional sources

varieties.name: Red June
varieties.origin: Early-season variety introduced to India
varieties.traits: Medium to large (2.5 inches diameter), vibrant red skin
varieties.flesh_fruit: Firm, juicy, sweet flesh with good balance of sugar and acidity
varieties.yield_notes: Early season ripening, good producer
varieties.growing_notes: Known for vibrant red skin and sweet juicy flesh. Early-season variety valuable for Indian market. Good sugar-acid balance. Grown in various parts of India.
varieties.availability: Indian commercial nurseries

varieties.name: Florida Red (Flordared)
varieties.origin: University of Florida, introduced to north Indian plains
varieties.traits: Large fruit, almost red at maturity, attractive appearance
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow flesh, freestone, juicy, excellent mid-season fruit
varieties.yield_notes: Average yield 100 kg per tree, matures early June
varieties.growing_notes: Low-chill cultivar successfully introduced to north-Indian plains. Used as male parent in Indian hybridization programs. Mid-season variety extends harvest window. Good yields and quality fruit.
varieties.availability: Northern India agricultural stations

varieties.name: Punjab Red (Nectarine) 🌏
varieties.origin: Punjab, India - local nectarine selection
varieties.traits: Smooth skin nectarine, vigorous spreading growth, minimum stone weight (13.96g) giving 57.33g pulp
varieties.flesh_fruit: Smooth nectarine flesh, highest ascorbic acid (164.33 mg/kg), good flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Highest yield 23.5 kg per tree, matures second week of May, early bearer - late April/May harvest
varieties.growing_notes: Best performer in northern hill zone Chhattisgarh trials. Nectarine variation (smooth skin). Extremely high vitamin C content. Early bearing cultivar. Indigenous to Punjab region. Red variety grown commercially.
varieties.availability: Punjab nurseries, agricultural universities

varieties.name: TropicBeauty
varieties.origin: University of Florida & Texas A&M, 1989
varieties.traits: Round firm fruit, high red overcolor on bright yellow, very short fuzz
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep yellow melting flesh, semi-freestone, excellent sweetness
varieties.yield_notes: 150 chill hours, holds well on tree, highly productive
varieties.growing_notes: Industry standard for subtropical/tropical highlands. Evaluated successfully in Puerto Rico and south Florida. Could potentially work in Kerala hill stations with proper site selection. Parent line used in Taiwan subtropical breeding program.
varieties.availability: Would need import through agricultural research channels

varieties.name: TropicSnow
varieties.origin: University of Florida & Texas A&M, 1989
varieties.traits: 40-50% red blush over creamy white background
varieties.flesh_fruit: White melting flesh, semi-freestone, very low acid with excellent sweetness
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields, 90-97 day FDP

varieties.name: UFSun
varieties.origin: University of Florida
varieties.traits: Medium-sized, uniformly symmetrical, 50-60% red with darker red stripes
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow flesh, clingstone, sweet, sweetens longer on tree
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy annual crops, ultra-low 150 chill hours, FDP 80 days
varieties.growing_notes: Ultra-low chill makes it candidate for tropical trials. Non-melting flesh gives good shelf life. Proven in south Florida. Could be tested in Kerala conditions.
varieties.availability: Would require research import

varieties.name: Tainung No. 7 HongLing
varieties.origin: Taiwan Agricultural Research Institute (TARI), released 2020s, subtropical monsoon breeding
varieties.traits: Large fruit size, attractive appearance, bred specifically for subtropical monsoon climate
varieties.flesh_fruit: Low acidity characteristic, sweet flavor preferred for fresh consumption
varieties.yield_notes: Early flowering and harvesting, extremely low chilling requirement

varieties.name: Indian Blood (Cherokee Blood Cling)
varieties.origin: China (pre-11th century), brought to Mexico by Spanish 1500s, cultivated by Cherokee and Creek Native American tribes by 1600s. Despite name, not indigenous to India.
varieties.traits: Large fruit (up to 25cm diameter), dark crimson/scarlet red skin like beet, distinctive appearance
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow flesh with red streaks reaching to stone, crimson just below skin, clingstone, sweet when ripe with tart undertones, distinctive flavor unlike most peaches
varieties.yield_notes: High yield, very productive, late season (August), dependable
varieties.growing_notes: One of oldest heirloom peaches brought to Americas. Grown by Thomas Jefferson at Monticello (planted 1807), one of 38 types still at Monticello. Superior canning peach. Grows easily from seed (rare for peaches). Hardy and disease resistant. Rich in anthocyanins (antioxidants). Historical significance - first peaches Spanish missionaries brought to New World. Fragile when ripe. Self-fertile but needs pollinator (not self-fruitful). Pickles beautifully. Used in breeding programs. Medium-high chill requirement (500+ hours) - NOT suitable for Kerala tropical climate.
varieties.availability: Trees of Antiquity USA, Monticello historic collection

varieties.name: Elberta
varieties.origin: Georgia USA, developed by Samuel H. Rumph, 1875, named for his wife Clara Elberta Moore Rumph, possibly Chinese Cling seedling
varieties.traits: Large round fruit, golden-yellow skin with crimson blush, attractive appearance
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow flesh, freestone, sweet with delicate flavor, firm texture
varieties.yield_notes: Exceedingly prolific - one small tree can produce 200 peaches, larger trees more, hardy
varieties.growing_notes: 'QUEEN OF PEACHES' before Redhaven developed. Most important commercial peach 1875-1940s. Revolutionary for shipping - first peach firm enough for long-distance transport un-iced (trial shipment NY 1875). Adapted to all soils and climates Gulf to Great Lakes, Atlantic to Pacific. Foundation of Georgia peach industry and Michigan's 1890s industry. Still grown commercially. Poem written about it by Mabel Swartz Withoft 1900s. Historical significance - transformed American peach industry. Requires thinning. Late August/September harvest (California July-August). 600+ chill hours - NOT suitable for Kerala.
varieties.availability: Stark Bros, Grandpa's Orchard, Trees of Antiquity, heritage nurseries worldwide

varieties.name: Belle of Georgia (Georgia Belle)
varieties.origin: Marshallville Georgia, discovered by Lewis A. Rumph (Samuel Rumph's uncle), 1870, named for Mrs. Belle Hall, possibly Chinese Cling seedling
varieties.traits: Large fruit, greenish-yellow skin, creamy white flesh
varieties.flesh_fruit: White flesh, freestone, firm texture with honeyed sweetness, 'so juicy bathtub good place to eat one' (1949), excellent flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy producer, made it prominent peach by end of 19th century

varieties.name: Redhaven
varieties.origin: Michigan State University, developed by Dr. Stanley Johnston, released 1940, revolutionized eastern peach growing
varieties.traits: Round medium-sized fruit, red skin, attractive appearance
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow flesh, freestone, sweet flavor, firm texture
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive, hardy variety, good bacterial spot resistance

varieties.name: Hiley Belle (Hiley)
varieties.origin: Marshallville Georgia, introduced by Eugene Hiley, 1886
varieties.traits: White-fleshed peach with golden skin and red blush
varieties.flesh_fruit: White flesh, good flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer
varieties.growing_notes: Heavily planted throughout middle Georgia for several decades alongside Elberta and Belle of Georgia. Part of trio that defined Georgia peach industry late 1800s-early 1900s. Historical importance to American peach culture.
varieties.availability: Heritage collections, rare

varieties.name: Carman
varieties.origin: Historical variety, once favorite of Midwestern and Southern growers
varieties.traits: White peach, very rare heirloom
varieties.flesh_fruit: White flesh, bursting with sweetness, melts in mouth
varieties.yield_notes: Vigorous and productive
varieties.growing_notes: Extremely rare heirloom that shouldn't be lost. Grown as 'El Dulce' in southern California. Every bit as vigorous/productive now as historically. Once favorite white peach of era.
varieties.availability: Arboreum Company (currently sold out), extremely rare

varieties.name: J.H. Hale
varieties.origin: First fruit tree to receive plant patent, famous historical variety
varieties.traits: Large fruit
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow flesh, excellent quality
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer BUT not self-fertile - requires pollinator
varieties.growing_notes: Historical significance - first plant patent. Only variety that is NOT self-fruitful - must have another variety to pollinate. Important in peach breeding history.
varieties.availability: Grandpa's Orchard, heritage nurseries

varieties.name: Red Rareripe (Morris Red Rareripe)
varieties.origin: Old heirloom, likely grown by Thomas Jefferson at Monticello, multiple named selections (Prince's, Martin's, Pratt, Late Red)
varieties.traits: Small fruit (not surpassing 3.5 inches diameter), red blush sometimes on and under skin
varieties.flesh_fruit: Described as productive, melting, highly flavored, excellent, superior to supermarket varieties
varieties.yield_notes: Productive
varieties.growing_notes: Near-wild variety, grows true from seed (unique for peaches). Hardier than most peaches - survives Zone 5A. Early ripening historically, though ripens September in northern climates. Yellow and white varieties existed ('lemon rareripes'). Grows easily from pits with cold stratification. More tedious to process due to small size but superior flavor. Jefferson recommended 20ft apart for great production and firewood. Hundreds of seedlings possible from dropped fruit.
varieties.availability: Rare, mainly through seed savers, some heritage collections

varieties.name: Cresthaven
varieties.origin: Michigan State University, Dr. Stanley Johnston, released few years after Redhaven 1940s
varieties.traits: Developed for cold climates
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow flesh, freestone, excellent flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields, cold hardy
varieties.growing_notes: Follow-up to Redhaven success. Another heirloom peach from famous 'Haven' breeding program. Cold climate adaptation. Part of revolution in northern peach growing.
varieties.availability: Heritage nurseries, cold-climate specialists

varieties.name: Golden Jubilee
varieties.origin: Bred from Elberta, real heirloom variety
varieties.traits: Large late yellow peach
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow flesh, sweet rich flavor, good texture
varieties.yield_notes: Self-fertile, low-chill variety
varieties.growing_notes: Works well in South. Might be best variety for California and similar climates. Showy pink spring blossoms add ornamental value. Descended from famous Elberta.
varieties.availability: Arbor Day Foundation, heritage nurseries

varieties.name: Loring
varieties.origin: Missouri peach, 1950s+, grown across country
varieties.traits: Large fruit, pink spring blossoms
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow flesh, good for desserts and canning
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer, pretty adaptable
varieties.growing_notes: Sold at orchards in 1990s. Adaptable across country. Ornamental features with edible fruit perfect for edible gardens. Heirloom peach with wide geographic success.
varieties.availability: Heritage nurseries, Bennett Orchards

varieties.name: Dixieland
varieties.origin: USDA introduction to fill void when Elberta passion waned
varieties.traits: Large fruit, yellow skin with red blush
varieties.flesh_fruit: White flesh, sweet soft flesh
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for fresh eating or baking. Introduced to maintain peach industry when Elberta popularity declined. White-fleshed heirloom from USDA breeding.
varieties.availability: Heritage collections
